title: The gold medal for deception: How a professor created an international award out of thin air
subhead: In 2016, Florent Montaclair stepped into the spotlight when he received the Gold Medal of Philology, a sort of Nobel Prize for the discipline. In fact, the award was created by Montaclair himself. It took an investigation by Romanian journalists to uncover this hoax, which is now the subject of a judicial inquiry in France.

In Milan, Europe's far right gathers amid shifting era and enduring identity politics
subhead: Just days after Viktor Orban's defeat in Hungary, Matteo Salvini, Italy's deputy prime minister, hosted a far-right rally under the banner 'Without Fear: In Europe We Are Masters in Our Own House.'

title: Bulgarian legislative elections: Corruption and ties to Russia at the heart of the campaign
subhead: Leading in the polls, former president Rumen Radev, known for his ambiguous stance on Russia, has made headlines for his anti-corruption platform and promises to 'topple the oligarchy.' Sunday's vote marks the eighth election in five years.

title: Ireland, the weak link in Europe's defense
subhead: Irish neutrality, long seen as a guarantee of independence from its former British colonizer, has been eroding under European and Atlantic pressures. After decades of naval weakness, Dublin has committed to strengthening its navy.

title: From a South American frog to a Russian penal colony: How the poison epibatidine killed Navalny
subhead: This extremely powerful neurotoxin is secreted by a frog native to Ecuador. Although Western scientists discovered it in the 19th century, it wasn't synthesized until the 1990s. The neurotoxin appears to have particularly interested the Russian research institute responsible for synthesizing Novichok agents, used in poisonings.
byline: Audrey Lagadec,  Eric Dedier and  Rémi Ducasse

subhead: Following a visit by President John Dramani Mahama to the Elysée, Ghana's foreign affairs minister announced on April 8 that Paris would be willing to 'collaborate' on 'restorative justice.' Those close to Emmanuel Macron confirmed that the French president supported the initiative.

title: 'Viktor Orban's defeat in Hungary is truly a defeat for the reactionary international'
subhead: In an interview, Italian historian Steven Forti, a specialist in far-right movements, says that while Péter Magyar's victory is good news for the European Union, it remains unclear what alternative this conservative figure represents for the right – a question that resonates elsewhere.

title: Saint-Denis Mayor Bally Bagayoko: 'When you win Paris's largest suburb, it gives you a different standing'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, Bally Bagayoko says he can play a role in uniting the left around La France Insoumise leader Jean-Luc Mélenchon for the 2027 French presidential election.
